facts.text: The Old Kampe Post Office, built in 1773, sits at the corner of Canistear and Cherry Ridge Roads as Highland Lakes’ oldest landmark.
Highland Lakes was first developed in the 1930s as a summer retreat for families from the New York City metropolitan area.
The community surrounds five lakes—Highland, East Highland, Upper Highland, Upper West Highland, and Upper East Highland—offering seven beaches for residents.
Most homes are lake‑style or log cabins, preserving the original architecture that defines Highland Lakes’ character.
Every property owner pays an initiation fee and annual dues to the Highland Lakes Country Club and Community Association.
In 1935, John Seckler, Clayton Shepperd, William Dailey, and Clarkson Potter formed Highland Lakes, Inc., the development corporation that shaped the community.
